get.description:
Creates a long standing client-server websocket connection.

Note: The code sample provided is an auto-generated sample and is intended to demonstrate certain functionalities and may not be suitable for direct use.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: The access token obtained during authentication.

parameters.name: event_types
parameters.description: A comma separate list of alert events this connection will deliver.

timestamp.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp that should be used to validate the freshness of this message to protect against replay attacks. You should verify that this value is no older than 5 minutes from the current system time.

alert_id.description: Unique identifier of the specific alert.

id.description: Unique RSOS account identifier of the authorized entity responsible for this alert.

name.description: Name of the authorized entity responsible for this alert.

phone.description: Administrative phone number of this authorized entity

id.description: Unique RSOS account identifier of the PSAP with jurisdiction over this alert location.

name.description: Name of the PSAP with jurisdiction over this alert location.

phone.description: Administrative phone number of this PSAP

covering_psap_first_viewed_time.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of when this alert was first viewed by the psap.

created_time.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of when this alert record was created. This value should not be confused with `incident_time` which is the time when the incident the Alert represents actually occurred.

description.description: Short description of the Alert event provided by the source.
description.example: Break-in detected in zone 1 via door sensor 3.

dispatch_requested_time.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of when authorized entity (i.e. Central Station) requested dispatch from the covering PSAP

display_name.description: Human friendly name of the emergency type value

followers.description: User IDs of all CSP and RSP users who have taken action on this alert (chat, status update, etc.). This will always be empty for an alert.new event since no interactions have taken place yet.

incident_time.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of when the incident this Alert represents actually occurred.

is_chat_enabled.description: If this alert has chat enabled.

is_media_enabled.description: if this alert has media enabled.

last_updated_time.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of when the latest status change occurred

service_provider_name.description: Name of the service provider/dealer providing this Alert event

name.description: Internal value of the site type

display_name.description: Human friendly name of the site type value

sla_expiration_time.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of when the Alert must be accepted by the PSAP by, otherwise it will be considered timed out and unserviceable via the Alerts platform. This value will only be populated in `alert.new` events for ECC subscribers since there is no SLA determination until dispatch is requested by the Central Station.

source_id.description: Source provided identifier of the owner of this data.

name.description: Name of the current status value. This will always be `"NEW"` for `alert.new` events

display_name.description: Human-readable display label of the status value

supplemental_only.description: Flag indicating if this Alert is being used at the PSAP as supplemental data only. If `true` the PSAP cannot update status or chat and a traditional 10-digit adminstriative call is required for servicing the alert.

message.description: The message contents sent by the operator

name.description: Name of the disposition value the Alert has been updated with. This is the value you should use for any programmatic checks or tracking if necesary as it will always be the value that represents the given disposition.

display_name.description: Display name of the disposition value to be used as a suggested human-readable display label in UI elements. This value should not me used for any internal logic as it can change.

alert.location_update.title: alert.location_update

alert_id.description: Unique identifier of the Alert this event message is associated with

created_at.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of the server time this record was created

alert.location_update.description: Updates the location of an already-existing alert.

name.description: Name of the status value the Alert has been updated with. This is the value you should use for any programmatic checks or tracking if necesary as it will always be the value that represents the given status.

display_name.description: Display name of the status value to be used as a suggested human-readable display label in UI elements. This value should not me used for any internal logic as it can change.

sla_expiration_time.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of when the Alert must be accepted by the PSAP by, otherwise it will be considered timed out and unserviceable via the Alerts platform. This field is **only** populated when the status `name` attribute `"DISPATCH_REQUESTED"`

message.description: The message contents sent by the operator
The message contents sent by the operator

location_id.description: Unique identitifer for the location that associated with this event message

EventType.title: Event Type
EventType.description: Component which is the name of the event.

AlertUpdateEventCommonBody.title: Alert Update Common Body

alert_id.description: Unique identifier of the Alert this event message is associated with

created_at.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of the server time this record was created

entity_display_name.description: Display name of the entity (either Central Station or ECC) that the operator who generated this chat message belongs to

entity_id.description: Unique identifier of the the entity (either Central Station or ECC) that the operator who generated this chat message belongs to

message_time.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of the time this message was received. For chat messages this will always match the `created_time`.

sender.description: Name/username of the operator who sent this chat message

sender_id.description: Unique identifier of the operator who sent this chat message

AlertUpdateEventCommonBody.description: Conserved components of an Alert Update.

uncertainty_radius.description: Horizontal uncertainty in meters of the location provided by the location source device.

GeodeticCommonBody.description: Geodetic location information available for this event

name.description: Name of the location i.e. business name

street_1.description: Street address of the nearest civic location to the geolocation

street_2.description: Additional street address information such as apartment number or floor.

city.description: City or locality of the civic location

state.description: Code of state or region of the civic location

country.description: Country of the civic location up to 30 characters.

zip_code.description: Zip/postal code of the civic location

CivicCommonBody.description: Civic location information available for this event

ProvidedLocation.description: Indicates which location data object between `civic` and `geodetic` was provided by the source. This allows clients to determine if either value is an estimate provided by RapidSOS's geocoding/reverse-geocoding system.

EmergencyTypeValues.description: The type of emergency event that this alert describes.
